basics.name: David (Jiachen) Weng
basics.label: Community Engagement & Program Coordinator
basics.summary: Community engagement professional with 4+ years designing and delivering co-curricular programs, managing budgets, and building cross-campus partnerships at UBC. Bachelor of Media Studies graduate with firsthand understanding of the BMS program's pedagogical approach and community-engaged learning values. Experienced in event programming for 1,500+ residents, budget administration, cross-cultural facilitation, and educational content development. Bilingual (English & Mandarin) with strong cross-cultural sensitivity.

work.name: BC + AI Ecosystem
work.summary: Lead visual content operations for a growing AI community in British Columbia.
highlights: Designed 50+ branded assets for social media, events, and presentations reaching 3,000+ cumulative viewers
Managed multi-platform content publishing workflow across LinkedIn, Instagram, and YouTube, maintaining brand consistency across 3 channels

work.name: UBC Department of Physical Therapy
work.summary: Designed and developed inclusive e-learning content for the Physical Therapy program, applying Universal Design for Learning principles.
highlights: Designed and developed 5+ inclusive e-learning modules using Articulate 360 (Rise, Storyline), applying UDL principles for diverse learner needs
Collaborated with faculty to translate curriculum content into interactive digital formats serving 200+ students

work.name: UBC Student Housing and Hospitality Services
work.summary: Planned and delivered community-engaged learning programs for a residence community of 1,500+ students.
highlights: Designed and executed 12+ residential programs per academic year, including cross-cultural celebrations and educational workshops, engaging 25-60+ attendees per event
Administered weekly duty shifts including late-night safety rounds, enforcing campus policy and emergency response protocols for a community of 1,500+ residents

volunteer.summary: Managed all financial activities and pursued funding opportunities for the student association.
highlights: Administered budget, applied for grants, tracked expenses, and reported financials to executive team

volunteer.organization: UBC Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA)
highlights: Directed social media strategy and visual content production for events serving 1,000+ UBC students
Coordinated with executive team to promote cultural programming, driving event attendance and community engagement

projects.name: OpenKara
projects.description: Open-source desktop karaoke application using AI vocal separation and online synced lyrics.
highlights: Built with Tauri 2 + Rust, demonstrating technical project management and product development skills
